message:
docs+test: add comprehensive rustdoc and property-based tests across core, extent, and MVCC crates

The public API surface of ffs-core, ffs-extent, and ffs-mvcc lacked
documentation and had insufficient test coverage for concurrency edge
cases and extent manipulation invariants. This commit addresses both.

Documentation (ffs-core):
- Add rustdoc to all public types in the durability autopilot subsystem
  (DurabilityPosterior, DurabilityLossModel, RedundancyDecision,
  DurabilityAutopilot) explaining the Beta-Binomial model and cost
  trade-offs.
- Document FsFlavor, DetectionError, and all detect_filesystem* variants
  so callers understand the superblock probing flow.
- Add module-level doc comments for degradation and vfs submodules in
  ffs-core/lib.rs.
- Document FrankenFsEngine facade methods (begin, commit, snapshot,
  read, checkpoint, inspect_image, parse_ext4, parse_btrfs).
- Add doc comment to RequestScope::empty() in vfs.rs.

Style (ffs-cli):
- Collapse multi-line function signatures to single-line where the
  params fit within line-length limits (report_has_error_or_higher_for_block,
  build_ext4_repair_group_specs, build_repair_output, ext4_group_scrub_scope).
  Reduces visual noise without changing semantics.

Tests (ffs-extent):
- Add 6 proptest property-based tests for extent tree invariants:
  - Sub-range mapping returns correct physical offsets
  - Partial truncate preserves head and holes the tail
  - mark_written is idempotent on already-written extents
  - Disjoint allocations yield non-overlapping physical ranges
  - punch_hole creates visible holes while preserving prefix
  - split_for_mark_written produces exactly 1-3 extents

Tests (ffs-mvcc):
- Add 6 proptest property-based tests for MVCC concurrency invariants:
  - SSI detects write-skew (rw-antidependency cycles)
  - N concurrent writers to same block: exactly one wins under FCW
  - prune_safe preserves active snapshot visibility
  - Latest version survives pruning (GC correctness)
  - Multi-block transaction atomicity (all-or-nothing)
  - Interleaved serial transactions: no lost updates
- Add 20+ targeted unit tests for conflict detection edge cases:
  - FCW boundary semantics (write at vs after snapshot.high)
  - FCW conflict error field correctness
  - FCW three-way concurrent conflict
  - FCW disjoint blocks all succeed
  - SSI read-only transactions never conflict
  - SSI write-skew on read block
  - SSI FCW takes precedence over SSI
  - SSI self-read-write no conflict
  - SSI overlapping read-write set detection
  - SSI log boundary at snapshot.high
  - Empty transaction commits under FCW and SSI
  - Chain backpressure rejection at critical length with snapshot pin
  - Chain pressure without pin allows commit
  - Chain pressure force-advance exercises recovery path
  - SSI log pruning with exact boundary
  - Versions eligible at watermark (empty, single, multi-version)
  - latest_commit_seq tracking
  - critical_chain_len exceeds cap values
  - Concurrent read-only SSI transactions

Beads issue tracker: update status for 6 issues to reflect current state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

## GOAL
Increase test coverage for the two weakest modules in ffs-repair:
- storage.rs: 5 tests / 974 LOC (1:195 ratio)
- recovery.rs: 6 tests / 915 LOC (1:153 ratio)

## TEST TARGETS

### storage.rs
- Dual-slot descriptor switchover, torn write recovery, generation monotonicity
- Raw vs packed symbol modes, CRC validation round-trips
- Layout validation for edge-case block group sizes

### recovery.rs
- Partial recovery, full corruption, noop recovery
- Evidence record emission, post-repair verification failure

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- storage.rs: ≥12 tests (from 5), recovery.rs: ≥12 tests (from 6)

 {"id":"bd-2ad","title":"Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)","description":"# Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)

## GOAL
Implement robust mount lifecycle management with clean startup, shutdown, and signal handling.

## BACKGROUND
Production filesystems must handle:
- Clean mount with validation
- Graceful unmount with flush
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Crash recovery on restart

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Phase
```rust
pub async fn mount(config: MountConfig, cx: &Cx) -> Result<MountHandle> {
    // 1. Validate image (superblock, journal state)
    // 2. Replay journal if needed (ext4)
    // 3. Initialize MVCC store
    // 4. Initialize ARC cache
    // 5. Initialize repair manager
    // 6. Start background tasks (scrub, GC)
    // 7. Mount FUSE session
    // 8. Return handle for lifecycle control
}
```

### 2. Unmount Phase
```rust
impl MountHandle {
    pub async fn unmount(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Stop accepting new requests
        // 2. Wait for in-flight requests (with timeout)
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries
        // 4. Commit pending transactions
        // 5. Refresh repair symbols for dirty groups
        // 6. Close FUSE session
        // 7. Sync and close device
    }
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: Trigger graceful unmount
- SIGUSR1: Dump debug state to log
- Timeout for unmount (default 30s, then force)

### 4. Drop Safety
- MountHandle::drop must trigger unmount
- Panic in unmount must not corrupt filesystem
- Leaked handle must eventually timeout

## TESTS
1. Unit: Mount/unmount roundtrip
2. Integration: SIGTERM during I/O results in clean state
3. Integration: Double-mount same image fails cleanly
4. Stress: Mount/unmount loop 100x without leaks

## LOGGING
- Mount: log config, image path, mount point
- Unmount: log flush stats, pending ops
- Signal: log signal received, action taken

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount ... && ffs umount ...` leaves clean state
2. `kill -TERM $(pidof ffs)` results in clean unmount
3. Remount after crash shows no corruption
4. No resource leaks (file descriptors, memory)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:34.171965858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310382109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310314994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","lifecycle","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:06.032850244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.263209229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2ad","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This bead covers the 'FUSE mount runtime' row (currently ❌).  {"id":"bd-2qf","title":"Repair: Extract FrankenSQLite RaptorQ approach and adapt to filesystem blocks","description":"Goal: reuse proven strategy from /dp/frankensqlite for fountain-code-based self-healing, but adapted to ext4/btrfs block groups and filesystem workloads.

Deliverables:

- Identify what changes for filesystems: block group boundaries, metadata vs data, read/write patterns.
- Update COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC repair section with an explicit design derived from this extraction.

Acceptance:
- We can explain exactly how a corrupted block is detected and which symbols are used to repair it.
- The design includes tunable parameters and an expected-loss justification (not ad-hoc thresholds).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:15.796807569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555409837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555343092Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-2r8","title":"Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation","description":"# Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation

## GOAL
Implement Cx-based budget system that propagates backpressure through the stack, preventing cascade failures under load.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
Traditional systems fail abruptly under load. FrankenFS must degrade gracefully using information-theoretic principles:

1. **Budget = scarce resource allocation**
   - deadline: wall-clock timeout
   - poll_quota: async poll iterations allowed
   - cost_quota: abstract "work units" allowed
   - priority: relative importance

2. **Budget combination**: min for limits, max for priority

3. **Backpressure = signal to reduce demand**
   When budget exhausted, callers must either:
   - Retry with fresh budget (if transient)
   - Propagate failure (if permanent)
   - Shed load (if graceful degradation enabled)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Budget Types
```rust
pub struct Budget {
    pub deadline: Option<Instant>,
    pub poll_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub cost_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub priority: Priority,
}

impl Budget {
    pub fn combine(&self, other: &Budget) -> Budget {
        Budget {
            deadline: min_option(self.deadline, other.deadline),
            poll_quota: min_option(self.poll_quota, other.poll_quota),
            cost_quota: min_option(self.cost_quota, other.cost_quota),
            priority: max(self.priority, other.priority),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.deadline.map_or(false, |d| Instant::now() > d)
            || self.poll_quota == Some(0)
            || self.cost_quota == Some(0)
    }
}
```

### 2. Integration Points
- **FUSE dispatch**: Per-request budget from config
- **ARC cache**: Eviction budget (prevent eviction storms)
- **MVCC commit**: Transaction budget
- **Scrub/GC**: Background budget (yields to foreground)
- **RaptorQ decode**: Symbol budget

### 3. Pressure Detection
```rust
pub struct PressureMonitor {
    pub fn sample_cpu_load() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_memory_pressure() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_io_depth() -> usize;
    pub fn compute_degradation_level() -> DegradationLevel;
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation FSM
```
Normal -> Pause Background -> Reduce Cache -> Throttle Writes -> Read-Only
   ^                                                              |
   +--------------------(pressure relieved)----------------------+
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Budget combination semantics
2. Unit: Pressure detection thresholds
3. Integration: Simulated CPU load triggers degradation
4. Integration: Recovery when pressure relieved

## LOGGING
- Budget exhaustion (warn)
- Degradation level change (info)
- Pressure samples (trace)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Operations respect budget limits
2. Degradation level reflects actual pressure
3. Recovery is automatic and stable
4. No infinite loops or deadlocks under pressure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:09.209435721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741671654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741640536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","perf","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.370992350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.081031452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.977623160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Initial slice: wire actionable write-side behavior and verification pass semantics.","notes":"Added bootstrap btrfs recovery for fsck/repair detection path: when initial detect fails and writes are allowed, CLI now restores primary superblock from validated backup mirror, re-detects, and continues; fsck repair status now reports bootstrap-performed; added two bootstrap regression tests; updated parity/readme wording.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-20T19:15:57.523409335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:11:30.754252131Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:09:58.460084040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","parity","repair"],"comments":[{"id":271,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented first slice: btrfs --block-group scoped scrub support in fsck/repair using extent-tree discovery and logical->physical mapping; added mapping unit tests; full btrfs write-side recovery remains open.","created_at":"2026-02-20T19:30:44Z"},{"id":272,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented next slice in crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs: btrfs backup superblock mirror repair from primary for both repair and fsck --repair paths; added post-write re-scrub verification and two regression tests for mirror restoration.  {"id":"bd-2s4","title":"EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime","description":"# EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime

## PURPOSE
Transform the FUSE interface from scaffold to production-ready mount runtime, as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gap #3.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §14 (FUSE Integration):
- Multi-threaded dispatch required
- All state must be Send + Sync
- Never block FUSE dispatch thread on raw I/O
- Proper lifecycle management (mount/unmount/signal handling)

Current state: "FUSE mount runtime: ❌ Interface scaffold only"

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Lifecycle
- Clean mount with explicit options (ro/rw, cache_size, etc.)
- Graceful unmount with flush and cleanup
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT -> clean unmount)
- Crash recovery on restart

### 2. Multi-threaded Dispatch
- Use fuser multi-threaded mode
- Thread pool sizing based on CPU count
- Per-thread ARC cache access (Send + Sync)
- Connection pooling for concurrent requests

### 3. Error Handling
- Every FUSE op maps to correct errno
- Timeout handling for hung operations
- Partial failure recovery

### 4. Performance
- Request batching where applicable
- Readahead for sequential access
- Writeback coalescing
- Attribute caching with configurable TTL

### 5. Observability
- Per-op latency metrics
- Request queue depth
- Error rate tracking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount --rw /path/to/image.ext4 /mnt/ffs` works in production
2. Concurrent readers/writers do not deadlock
3. `umount /mnt/ffs` is clean (no data loss)
4. SIGTERM results in clean shutdown
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping

## FILES
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (main implementation)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/mount.rs (lifecycle)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/dispatch.rs (multi-thread)
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (mount command)

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E suite (bd-3en1) passes
3. ffs mount --rw works on ext4 and btrfs images
4. SIGTERM unmount is clean (no data loss)
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:01.826764621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072572592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072549739Z","close_reason":"Completed: all child beads closed and acceptance criteria validated by latest E2E artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuse","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-32yn","title":"EPIC: Testing & Fault Injection","description":"# EPIC: Testing & Fault Injection

## PURPOSE
Build a comprehensive fault injection and stress testing framework for FrankenFS. A filesystem that cannot prove it handles failures correctly is not production-worthy. This epic covers: WAL interruption, partial repair, metadata corruption, I/O throttling, crash-replay testing, fsx-style stress, and xfstests compatibility.

## BACKGROUND
Kernel filesystems (ext4, btrfs, XFS) have decades of testing infrastructure: xfstests, fsx, blktests, syzkaller. FrankenFS must build equivalent coverage, with the added dimension of testing its unique features (MVCC, RaptorQ self-healing).

## SCOPE
1. Fault injection primitives (pluggable I/O layer with configurable failures)
2. WAL interruption tests (crash during journal write)
3. Partial repair tests (incomplete RaptorQ symbols)
4. Metadata corruption tests (corrupt specific metadata structures)
5. I/O throttling tests (slow I/O, deadline misses)
6. Crash-replay suite (deterministic crash schedules)
7. fsx-style stress (random operations + verification)
8. xfstests compatibility (run standard FS test suites)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Fault injection layer can inject failures at any I/O point
- [ ] All fault injection tests are deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Crash-replay covers 500+ random schedules
- [ ] fsx-style stress runs for configurable duration with corruption checking
- [ ] xfstests generic + ext4 subsets pass

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. Fault injection framework reusable across all scenarios
3. xfstests subset passes (bd-32yn.7)
4. Crash-replay suite covers 500+ schedules
5. CI-executable without hardware dependencies","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:46.527032547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:26:39.992566877Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:26:39.992541059Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["faultinjection","reliability","testing"],"comments":[{"id":92,"issue_id":"bd-32yn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-32yn.1","title":"Fault injection: WAL interruption (crash mid-fsync)","description":"# Fault injection: WAL interruption (crash mid-fsync)

## GOAL
Test filesystem behavior when a crash occurs during WAL/journal write (mid-fsync). This is the most critical crash scenario — partial journal records must be handled correctly.

## SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Crash during WAL entry write (before fsync)
- Begin transaction, write WAL entries, crash before fsync
- Expected: on recovery, transaction is cleanly aborted (no partial data)

### Scenario 2: Crash during fsync (partial write to disk)
- WAL entry partially written (torn write)
- Expected: CRC32C detects torn entry, entry discarded, transaction aborted

### Scenario 3: Crash after fsync but before commit record
- WAL data entries durable, but commit record not written
- Expected: transaction aborted (no commit = no visibility)

### Scenario 4: Crash during commit record write
- Commit record partially written
- Expected: CRC32C of commit record fails, transaction aborted

### Scenario 5: Crash after commit record (before cache flush)
- Transaction committed in WAL, but dirty cache blocks not flushed
- Expected: WAL replay reconstructs committed state

## IMPLEMENTATION
- Pluggable I/O layer with injectable failure points
- Failure injection: fail_at_byte(offset) — crash after writing N bytes
- Deterministic: same seed produces same crash point
- Recovery verification: mount after crash, verify filesystem consistent

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 5 scenarios produce correct recovery behavior
- [ ] No data corruption from partial WAL writes
- [ ] No silent data loss
- [ ] Deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Evidence records logged for all recovery actions","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:59.368753493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:41:02.489924878Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:41:02.489856841Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","fault","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:32.516893629Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-32yn.1","depends_on_id":"bd-32yn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:27:59.368753493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-tnu","title":"Docs: Add AGENTS.md playbooks (cass rituals + alien artifact + optimization)","description":"Goal: make agent workflow more repeatable by adding short, high-signal playbooks to AGENTS.md.

- Alien-artifact elicitation prompt + evidence-ledger / loss-matrix reminder.

- Porting-to-rust essence extraction checklist (spec-first, conformance-first).

Acceptance:
- AGENTS.md gains a new Playbooks section with copy-paste-ready snippets.
